messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "/compact command hangs indefinitely in Agent Host sessions",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "command", "hang", "blocking"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a user in an Agent Host session, I want the /compact command to complete within a reasonable time frame so that I can manage long conversation history without being stuck waiting indefinitely.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a user has typed /compact and pressed Enter, When the command executes Then it must either complete successfully or timeout after X seconds with an error message",
    "Given the /compact command is running in background, When the conversation history reaches compression threshold Then compact operation should start automatically or be offered as option",
    "Given a user runs /compact on a session approaching context limit, When the operation completes Then the session token usage should reflect compressed state",
    "Given /compact hangs for more than 30 seconds, When user presses Ctrl+C Then it should gracefully cancel and show cancellation message"
  ],
  "story_points": null,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Locate the /compact command implementation in agent host codebase",
      "Analyze why compact operation hangs - check for missing timeout or infinite loop",
      "Add proper timeout mechanism (30-60 seconds) with cancellation support",
      "Implement error handling and user feedback during hang detection"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update command output to show progress/status during compact operation",
      "Display timeout/cancellation message when /compact is interrupted"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test /compact completes successfully on short sessions",
      "Verify timeout behavior after 30+ seconds of hanging",
      "Confirm Ctrl+C cancellation works properly",
      "Validate token usage reflects compression after successful compact"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Bug fixed and verified", "Timeout mechanism implemented", "User feedback added", "Code reviewed"],
  "planning_status": "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION",
  "clarification_questions": [
      "Where is the /compact command implementation located in the codebase? The provided snippets show session management but not the compact handler.",
      "What is the expected behavior of /compact - does it compress all history or only recent messages?",
      "Are there any existing timeout patterns in other agent host commands we can reference?",
      "Is this a regression from a previous working version, or new behavior?"
    ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The /compact command exists and is documented as a valid feature",
    "Other long-running operations in agent host have proper timeout handling",
    "Users expect commands to complete within reasonable timeframes"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md"]
}
```
